description: Curated allow-list of high-SWE models proven good for agentic coding, with per-workflow suitability. The public pricing corpus has no quality dimension, so this overlay is the source of capability + workflow routing. Entries are matched against a model id by `match` substrings (case-insensitive); the FIRST pattern in this ordered list that matches wins, so most-specific patterns come first. Fields: swe = approx SWE-bench Verified solve-rate (0..100, used as the corpus capability score); single_pass = suitability for one-shot agentic authoring (zoder oneshot / exec), 0..1; grind = suitability for the adversarial 'grind until green' loop (zoder loop), 0..1. grind weights multi-step RUNTIME-failure convergence, not one-shot quality: a model can author well yet stall when a check fails on runtime behavior it must debug iteratively.
evidence: single_pass ranks one-shot code quality; grind ranks loop convergence. Empirical (ncz-os, 2026-06): Claude (Opus/Sonnet) and DeepSeek-R1-class reasoners converge on runtime-debug grinds (e.g. the ncz-persist M2 sqlx backends, fixed first-pass by a Claude agent after the open-weight loop stalled). Kimi-K2 authored compiling backends but could NOT debug the runtime conformance failure — it spun a non-progressing 1-line diff until the no-progress guard stopped it; so Kimi is high single_pass / low grind. gpt-oss is similar (good cheap author, weak grind). Compile-gated grinds (clear localized errors) converge for far more models than runtime-gated ones.

patterns.note: near-Opus convergence, cheaper; default strong grinder
reasoning model; strong multi-step runtime-debug grind
strong SINGLE-PASS; weak runtime-debug grind (ncz-persist M2 stall, 2026-06)
Moonshot Kimi fallback; prefer for single-pass not grind
Zhipu GLM; good author, moderate grind
zoder default author; cheap; moderate grind
NVIDIA Nemotron (EIH); solid author, moderate grind
free via groq; good cheap author, weak grind
